userStories.title: nax diagnose CLI — pure pattern-matching diagnosis
userStories.description:
Implement the `nax diagnose` command that reads existing run artifacts (events.jsonl from ~/.nax/events/<project>/, status.json from <workdir>/.nax-status.json, PRD from nax/features/<feature>/prd.json, nax.lock from <workdir>/nax.lock, git log) and produces a structured human-readable diagnosis report. No LLM calls. No agent. Purely deterministic pattern matching.

COMMAND SIGNATURE:
  nax diagnose [-f <feature>] [-d <workdir>] [--json] [--verbose]

OUTPUT SECTIONS:
1. Run Summary — feature, last run time, status (complete/stalled/failed), stories passed/failed/pending, cost, commits produced
2. Story Breakdown — per-story: status, attempts, tier, strategy, pattern detected
3. Failure Analysis — for each failed story: pattern name, symptom description, fix suggestion
4. Lock Check — is nax.lock present? Is PID alive (process.kill(pid, 0))? Show fix command if stale.
5. Recommendations — ordered list of next actions

FAILURE PATTERNS TO DETECT (match in order):
- GREENFIELD_TDD: failureCategory === greenfield-no-tests in priorErrors or events
- TEST_MISMATCH: 2+ consecutive tests-failing escalation events for same story
- ENVIRONMENTAL: precheck-failed event OR blocker in status.json
- RATE_LIMITED: rateLimited: true in agent session failure events
- ISOLATION_VIOLATION: failureCategory === isolation-violation
- MAX_TIERS_EXHAUSTED: story has priorErrors for all configured tiers AND status is failed/paused
- SESSION_CRASH: session-failure category + no commits for that story in git log
- STALLED: isStalled flag in status.json OR all pending stories blocked by dependencies
- LOCK_STALE: nax.lock present + PID not alive
- AUTO_RECOVERED: story had greenfield-no-tests but status is passed (S5 worked)
- UNKNOWN: no pattern matched

GRACEFUL DEGRADATION: If events.jsonl is missing, fall back to PRD priorErrors + git log only. Never crash — always produce partial output with a note about missing data.

FILES TO CREATE:
- src/cli/diagnose.ts — main implementation (read artifacts, pattern match, format output)
- src/commands/diagnose.ts — thin commander wrapper registering the command
- Register in bin/nax.ts — add diagnose command import
- test/integration/cli-diagnose.test.ts — integration tests covering all ACs
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: `nax diagnose` reads the last run for the current directory feature and prints human-readable output with all 5 sections (Run Summary, Story Breakdown, Failure Analysis, Lock Check, Recommendations)
AC2: Each failed story shows a pattern classification from the known patterns list, or UNKNOWN if no match
AC3: Stale nax.lock (lock present + PID dead) is detected and a fix command shown: rm nax.lock
AC4: `--json` flag outputs machine-readable JSON containing the same data as human output
AC5: Works gracefully when events.jsonl is missing — uses PRD + git log only, prints a note about missing events
AC6: `-f <feature>` targets a specific feature; `-d <workdir>` sets working directory
AC7: AUTO_RECOVERED stories (S5 switched strategy, story passed) are shown as INFO not ERROR
AC8: TypeScript compiles cleanly (bun x tsc --noEmit)
priorErrors: Attempt 1 failed with model tier: balanced
